name: Poached Pears with Rainforest Rice Cakes
recipeIngredient: 6 Medium Bartlett Pears, ripe but firm
1 Tablespoon Unsalted Cashew Nuts, coarsely chopped
3/4 Cup Short-Grained Pearl Rice (Japanese sweet rice)
recipeInstructions:
Poach the pears:
1. In a medium non-corrosive saucepan combine the water, sugar, lemon slices, and vanilla bean.
2. Peel the pears and using a small scoop or teaspoon, carefully scoop out the bloom end and the core of each pear. Place each pear immediately into the water/sugar mixture to prevent browning. Repeat with the remaining pears.
3. Bring the pears to a boil over medium-high heat, while stirring gently. Reduce the heat to low. Cover the pears with a clean dish towel to keep them submerged. Simmer for about 20 minutes or until the pears are tender when pierced with a paring knife. Remove the pears and syrup to a medium bowl and cool completely. Refrigerate until well chilled.
Make the rice cakes:
1. Heat a medium noncorrosion saucepan over medium-high heat. Add the nuts and stir for 1 1/2 minutes to toast them. Add the milk, rice, sugar, and salt and bring to a boil, stirring occasionally.
2. Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer gently, stirring often, for 35 to 40 minutes or until the rice is very soft and absorbs all of the milk. The rice will be very sticky. Stir often during the last 10 minutes of cooking so the rice doesn't burn.
3. When the rice is cooked, transfer it to a medium bowl and stir in the diced fruits and vanilla. Set the bowl in a larger bowl filled with ice water to cool.
Make the raspberry sauce:
1. In a medium noncorrosion saucepan, combine the raspberries and sugar. Cook over medium-low heat, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon until the sugar dissolves and the berries soften.
2. Remove the pan from the heat. Strain the raspberry mixture through a fine sieve into a bowl. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate the sauce.
Assemble the dessert:
1. Flatten one of the chilled rice balls to a patty that is 1 inch high. Place it in the center of a plate and set one of the poached pears on top. Spoon 2 to 3 tablespoons of the sauce around the plate. Repeat with the remaining rice patties and poached pears.
6 Servings
Preparation: 2 hours, plus cooling time
